descriptionEn: Effectively ended the Thirty Years' War by advocating religious toleration.
articleEn: The Treaty of Westphalia ended the Thirty Years' War in 1648. There was no real winner between the Catholic and Protestant forces. Rather, the Treaty of Westphalia promoted the idea of religious toleration. As a result, Catholicism, Calvinism, and Lutheranism were all legally recognized religions in their respected areas.

images.caption: The celebration of the Peace of Münster (a part of the Peace of Westphalia), Bartholomeus van der Helst, 1648
